title: Right Against Arrest Without Cause
description: Article 22 of the Indian Constitution protects individuals from arrest without lawful authority. No person shall be arrested or detained without being informed of the grounds for arrest or right to consult a lawyer. Protection provided during police custody.

title: Bail in Non-Cognizable Cases
description: Section 437 CrPC allows bail for persons accused of non-cognizable offences. The accused has a right to bail unless special circumstances exist. Police cannot detain such persons beyond 24 hours.

title: Anticipatory Bail
description: Section 438 CrPC allows an accused to apply for bail in anticipation of arrest. This bail is granted before actual arrest. Applicant must satisfy the court about their circumstances and likelihood of arrest.

title: Bond to be Executed in Non-Bailable Cases
description: Section 440 CrPC deals with bond execution. In non-bailable cases, if bail is granted, the person must execute a bond with sureties as the court directs.

title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
description: Section 41 IPC covers causing physical hurt. Hurting means causing bodily pain. Punishment extends to 6 months imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 500.

title: Culpable Homicide Not Amounting to Murder
description: Section 299 IPC defines culpable homicide. This applies when death is caused by rash or negligent act without intention to cause death or knowledge of likely consequences.

title: Punishment for Murder
description: Section 302 IPC defines murder and punishment. Murder means intentional causing of death. Punishment is life imprisonment or death penalty, with fine.

title: Assault or Criminal Force with Intent to Ourage Modesty
description: Section 354 IPC covers unwanted physical contact with intent to outrage modesty. Common in harassment cases. Punishment up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine.

title: Rape
description: Section 375 IPC defines rape. Non-consensual sexual intercourse is a serious crime. Punishment under Section 376 extends to life imprisonment.

title: Cruelty by Husband or In-laws
description: Section 498A IPC protects married women from cruelty. Covers physical and mental harassment. Cognizable offence, bailable with court's discretion.

title: Criminal Intimidation
description: Section 506 IPC covers threatening someone with injury to person, reputation or property. Punishment up to 2 years imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 1000.

title: Cheating
description: Section 420 IPC covers dishonestly inducing delivery of property. Common in fraud cases. Punishment up to 7 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Criminal Breach of Trust
description: Section 406 IPC applies when someone entrusted with property misappropriates or converts it. Punishment up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine.

title: Acts Done by Several Persons in Furtherance of Common Intention
description: Section 34 IPC holds all persons liable who act in furtherance of common intention. Each person is liable as if they individually committed the offence.

title: Act of Person in Unlawful Assembly
description: Section 149 IPC makes members of unlawful assembly liable. If common object of assembly is committed, each member is liable.

title: Examination of Complaint by Police
description: Section 156 CRPC gives police authority to investigate cognizable offences without permission. Police can start investigation on their own.

title: Examination of Witness by Police
description: Section 161 CRPC allows police to examine any person. Statements recorded are not admissible in court unless witness testifies.

title: Cautioning Witness
description: Section 162 CRPC states police statements to witnesses. These are recorded by police, not submitted as court evidence, but used to corroborate testimony.

title: When Police Report Need Not Be Furnished
description: Section 165 CRPC exempts certain matters from police reports. Court can proceed without investigating if satisfied.

title: Examination of Complainant
description: Section 202 CRPC allows magistrate to examine complainant before FIR is registered. Magistrate determines if case should proceed to investigation.

title: Dismissal of Complaint
description: Section 203 CRPC allows magistrate to dismiss complaint if insufficient ground for investigation. Court must record reasons.

title: Attendance of Witnesses
description: Section 212 CRPC empowers magistrate to compel witness attendance. Court can issue warrant to bring unwilling witnesses.

title: Examination Must Be Recorded
description: Section 251 CRPC mandates examination be recorded in detail. Statement must show what witness said, not magistrate's summary.

title: Examination of Accused
description: Section 313 CRPC provides right of examination to accused. Accused can be examined on evidence, must be informed they are not bound to say anything.

title: Burden of Proof
description: Article 41 Evidence Act defines burden of proof. Prosecution must prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt. Accused is presumed innocent until proven guilty.

title: Burden of Proof in Certain Cases
description: Article 105 Evidence Act shifts burden in certain cases. When accused raises doubt, prosecution must prove guilt to overcome it.

title: Production of Original Document
description: Article 162 Evidence Act covers original document production. Original must be produced unless exception applies.

title: Copies of Documents
description: Article 164 Evidence Act allows copies in certain cases. Certified copies are admissible when original is unavailable.

title: Punishment for Penetrative Sexual Assault on Child
description: POCSO Act Section 67 covers sexual assault on children. Serious offence with severe punishment including life imprisonment.

title: Punishment for Aggravated Penetrative Sexual Assault
description: POCSO Act Section 71 covers aggravated sexual assault. Higher punishment when violence or serious injury involved.

title: Anticipatory Bail
description: BNSS Section 438 is replacement for CrPC 438. Allows bail application before arrest. Modern version with updated procedures.

title: Public Nuisance
description: Section 268 IPC covers acts causing public inconvenience or injury. Punishment up to 3 months imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 250.

title: Obscene Acts and Songs
description: Section 294 IPC covers indecent exposure and obscene words in public. Punishment up to 3 months imprisonment and/or fine.

title: Waging or Attempting to Wage War Against Government
description: Section 153 IPC is a serious offence. Covers attempting to overthrow government. Punishment can be life imprisonment.

title: Giving False Evidence
description: Section 181 IPC covers deliberately giving false evidence in court. Punishment up to 7 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Punishment for Giving False Evidence
description: Section 193 IPC specifies punishment. If false evidence causes wrongful conviction, punishment extends to life imprisonment.

title: Act Endangering Life or Personal Safety
description: Section 336 IPC covers rash acts endangering safety. Punishment up to 3 months imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 250.

title: Causing Hurt by Act Endangering Life
description: Section 337 IPC covers causing hurt through rash act. Punishment up to 6 months imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 500.

title: Causing Disappearance of Evidence
description: Section 201 IPC covers destroying evidence. Punishment up to 7 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Intentional Insult with Knowledge that Will Provoke
description: Section 228 IPC covers intentional insults. Punishment up to 6 months imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 500.

title: Criminal Trespass
description: Section 447 IPC covers unauthorized entry into property. Punishment up to 3 months imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 500.

title: Theft
description: Section 380 IPC defines theft. Taking property with intent to permanently deprive owner. Punishment up to 3 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Punishment for Theft
description: Section 379 IPC specifies punishment. Covers various types of theft offences with different punishments.

title: Punishment for Dacoity
description: Section 392 IPC covers dacoity (5+ people stealing). Serious offence. Punishment up to 10 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Causing Death by Negligence
description: Section 304 IPC covers death caused by negligence. Punishment up to 2 years imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 1000.

title: Dowry Harassment
description: Section 498-A IPC protects women from harassment related to dowry. Cognizable offence against husband and in-laws.

title: In Cases Where Act is Forbidden and Wrongfully Restrained
description: Section 21 IPC covers wrongful restraint. Punishment varies based on severity of restraint and duration.

title: Criminal Intimidation
description: Section 503 IPC covers threatening with injury. Punishment up to 2 years imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs. 1000.

title: Criminal Intimidation by Anonymous Communication
description: Section 506 IPC covers threats by anonymous means. Punishment up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine.

title: Punishment for Insulting Religion
description: Section 67 IPC covers deliberate intention to outrage religious feelings. Punishment up to 3 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Injuring or Defiling Place of Worship
description: Section 295 IPC covers damaging religious places. Punishment up to 2 years imprisonment and fine up to Rs. 1000.

title: Maintenance of Wife Children and Parents
description: Section 125 CRPC covers maintenance obligations. Non-bailable offence, civil remedy for maintenance.

title: Act of Drunken Person
description: Section 52 IPC covers acts done while involuntarily intoxicated. Person may be exempted if intoxication prevents knowledge of act.

title: Act of Person Who by Reason of Insanity Did Not Know Nature of Act
description: Section 66 IPC provides insanity defence. Person unaware of act's nature/quality due to insanity may not be liable.

title: Act of Person Who Reasonably Believes Act Lawful
description: Section 92 IPC covers good faith belief in lawfulness. If person reasonably believes act is lawful, may escape liability.

title: Act Which Would be Lawful but for Mistake
description: Section 96 IPC covers mistakes. If act would be lawful without mistake, no offence if mistake is reasonable.

title: Procedure When Accused Admits Guilt
description: Section 503 CRPC allows accused to plead guilty. Court records plea and may proceed to sentencing.

title: Case to Be Tried by Magistrate
description: Section 356 CRPC determines trial court. Various offences tried by different grades of magistrates.

title: Conviction on Plea of Guilty
description: Section 408 CRPC covers conviction when guilty plea recorded. Court must be satisfied of voluntariness.
